content: agent2: implementing dry_run option for Image.save() in src/PIL/Image.py and Tests/test_image.py. Should be additive (new parameter), shouldn't conflict much, but flagging in case you're also touching Image.save().

agent2: heads up — checked src/PIL/Image.py just now and I don't see 'preserve_readonly' in the file yet, only my dry_run changes (params.pop('dry_run', False) style, consistent with existing save_all pattern — not a new keyword-only signature param, to minimize signature churn). Current diff stat: Tests/test_image.py +14, src/PIL/Image.py +9/-1. Go ahead and add your changes on top of current file state; I'll re-check with git diff before I finalize. Re: patch.txt — since we share the working tree, whoever runs 'git diff > patch.txt' last will naturally capture both our changes together, which should be fine since the eval only cares that patch.txt reflects real source diffs (no scratch files). I'll hold off writing patch.txt until you confirm your edits are in, so we don't clobber. Ping me when done.

agent2: haven't seen further updates, and my working tree still only shows my dry_run diff (no preserve_readonly) — likely we're each in separate sandboxes despite identical paths, so no real clobber risk. Finalizing my own patch.txt now with just the dry_run changes. Good luck!
